questions.title: 1. Balance on One Leg - 20 Seconds Each Side
questions.instructions: Stand on one leg without shoes. Time yourself for 20 seconds each side. Arms can be out for balance.

options.label: A
options.text: I held it for 20 seconds on both legs with ease.

options.label: B
options.text: I held it, but wobbled or lost balance once or twice.

options.label: C
options.text: I couldn't hold it for 20 seconds or had to put my foot down several times.

questions.title: 2. Plank Hold - 20 Seconds (Straight Arms or Elbows)
questions.instructions: Get into a plank position with either straight arms or on your elbows. Keep your hips level and core tight.
questions.prompt: How did you feel during the plank?

options.label: A
options.text: I held a strong plank for 20 seconds with no shaking or pain.

options.label: B
options.text: I felt challenged but managed to hold it, with some shaking or arching.

options.label: C
options.text: I couldn't hold for 20 seconds or had pain in my back/shoulders.

questions.title: 3. Get Off the Floor Using One Hand
questions.instructions: Start seated or lying on the floor and try to stand up using only one hand for support.

options.label: A
options.text: I stood up smoothly using just one hand.

options.label: B
options.text: I managed but felt wobbly, or needed momentum.

options.label: C
options.text: I needed two hands or struggled to get up at all.

questions.title: 4. Single-Leg Glute Bridge - 30 Seconds Each Side
questions.instructions: Lie on your back, one foot flat on the floor, other leg extended. Lift your hips and hold.

options.label: A
options.text: I held each side for 30 seconds with level hips.

options.label: B
options.text: I held it but hips dropped slightly or I felt some strain.

options.label: C
options.text: I couldn't hold for 30 seconds or had discomfort/pain.

questions.title: 5. Chair Squats x10 (3s down, 3s up)
questions.instructions: Stand in front of a chair. Lower slowly in 3 seconds, then stand back up in 3 seconds. Repeat 10 times.
questions.prompt: How did you perform the squats?

options.label: A
options.text: I did all 10 reps with control, no pain or wobble.

options.label: B
options.text: I finished all reps but struggled with control, depth, or knees wobbled.

options.label: C
options.text: I had to stop early or had pain/discomfort doing them.

questions.title: 6. Thoracic Extension (Prone) - 20-Second Hold
questions.instructions: Lie on your stomach with arms out in front (like Superman). Lift your chest and arms off the floor and hold.
questions.prompt: Which best describes how it went?

options.label: A
options.text: I held it easily for 20 seconds, felt activation in upper back.

options.label: B
options.text: I held it but felt strain or couldn't keep chest lifted high.

options.label: C
options.text: I couldn't hold for 20 seconds or had pain in shoulders/back.

pass.title:
🥳 🎉 🥳 🎉 Congratulations 🥳 🎉 🥳 🎉

✅ AMS | Ground Zero™ - PASSED!
pass.body:
Great stuff. You've passed the Ground Zero Assessment. That means you've built enough foundation to take on the next level of strength, control, and mobility.

👉 Next step:
If you managed all exercises with ease then I would highly recommend that you try the AMS | ReBuild™ assessment quiz next and see where you land.

fail.title: 🟢 Recommended Level: AMS | Ground Zero™
fail.body:
Your movement assessment shows that AMS | Ground Zero™ is the right level for you. Aim to challenge yourself through the program.

This phase is about laying the right groundwork: building balance, control, and the foundational strength you can rely on as you progress up the chain.
